title: Polymath surface routing declaration
description: Validates a per-surface deterministic routing declaration. Each routable surface (skill, workflow, or — from Phase 3 — tool) declares its own signals in a sidecar `routing.yaml` next to the surface (skills/tools) or under plugins/polymath-flows/routing/<name>.yaml (workflows). tools/build-surface-index.py is the SINGLE PRODUCER that compiles every declaration into plugins/polymath-core/data/route-signals.json (formerly hand-maintained) so the polymath-core route-hint hook can narrow a prompt to the smallest matching surface. Hard signals (url/regex/paths/diff) are what actually fire a hint; intents alone never fire. `kind`, `id`, and the proposed `surface` string are DERIVED from the file location, not declared here, so a declaration cannot drift from the surface it routes to.

url.description: Regex patterns matched (case-insensitive) against URLs found in the prompt. Hard signal (score 3).

regex.description: Regex patterns matched against the raw prompt text (e.g. CVE / GHSA / ticket keys). Hard signal (score 3).

paths.description: Case-insensitive substrings whose presence in the prompt hints this surface (e.g. 'package.json', 'docs/adrs/'). Hard signal worth 2; since the proposal threshold is 3, a path fires only together with a matching intent (it satisfies the hard-signal requirement but is not sufficient alone). Paths may legitimately overlap across surfaces; scoring disambiguates.

diff.description: When true, the presence of an inline unified diff in the prompt hints this surface. Hard signal worth 2; like paths, it fires only together with a matching intent (threshold is 3).

intents.description: Naive natural-language phrasings that raise this surface's score (soft signal, score 1). Never fire on their own — a hard signal must also be present. Must be globally unique across all surfaces (the disambiguation floor, enforced by build-surface-index.py --strict).

alt.description: A single named alternative surface to offer alongside the primary proposal (e.g. the lighter skill behind a heavier workflow). Form '<plugin>:<surface>'.

capabilities.description: Capability ids this surface needs at run time (Phase 2+). Resolved against .polymath/capabilities.yaml. Vocabulary is the closed set from shared/schemas/capabilities.schema.json.

trust.description: Graduated autonomy (Phase 4) — DECLARED metadata; no executor consumes it yet, so every surface still proposes-first regardless of this value (see run-workflow SKILL.md and docs/plans/consolidation-and-dispatch.md Outcome). 'propose' (default) = detect and propose, never auto-run. 'auto-headless' = declares eligibility to run without confirmation in a non-interactive session (CI / cron / -p) once an executor honors the axis. 'auto' = reserved and currently NOT permitted: the TRUST-1 gate fails the build on any 'auto' surface (it needs a per-surface write-scope analysis that does not exist yet).

chainsTo.description: Surface names this surface naturally hands off to on completion (Phase 4 arc chaining), e.g. respondToIncident -> incidentRetroToActions.
